title: Tool with Structured Input
constraints: The tool must accept a structured address with separate fields (street, city, state, zip)
The agent must correctly populate all address fields from natural language input
The tool must validate that required fields are present
No address components should be concatenated into a single string
hints: Use a Pydantic model or TypedDict to define the structured input schema with typed fields
Update the tool's parameter signature to use the structured type instead of a flat string
Include field descriptions in the schema so the LLM knows what format each field expects

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_tool_calling_agent
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate
from langchain_core.tools import tool

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: The tool takes a flat string for the address, which the agent formats inconsistently.
# Sometimes it sends "123 Main St, Springfield, IL 62701", other times "Springfield IL 123 Main".
# The downstream API expects structured fields and parsing a flat string is error-prone.
# TODO: Replace the flat string with structured input (street, city, state, zip)

@tool
def validate_address(address: str) -> str:
    """Validate a shipping address."""
    # This tries to parse a flat string — fragile and error-prone
    parts = address.split(",")
    if len(parts) < 3:
        return f"Invalid address format: '{address}'. Expected: street, city, state zip"
    return f"Address validated: {address}"

@tool
def calculate_shipping(address: str, weight_lbs: float) -> str:
    """Calculate shipping cost to an address."""
    return f"Shipping {weight_lbs}lbs to '{address}': $12.99 (parsed address may be wrong)"

prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a shipping assistant. Help users validate addresses and calculate shipping costs."),
    ("human", "{input}"),
    ("placeholder", "{agent_scratchpad}"),
])

agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, [validate_address, calculate_shipping], prompt)
executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=[validate_address, calculate_shipping])

result = executor.invoke({"input": "Validate and get shipping cost for a 5lb package to 742 Evergreen Terrace, Springfield, IL 62704"})
print(result["output"])
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner
from agents.tool import function_tool

# BUG: The tool takes a flat string for the address, which the agent formats inconsistently.
# Sometimes it sends "123 Main St, Springfield, IL 62701", other times "Springfield IL 123 Main".
# The downstream API expects structured fields and parsing a flat string is error-prone.
# TODO: Replace the flat string with structured input (street, city, state, zip)

@function_tool
def validate_address(address: str) -> str:
    """Validate a shipping address."""
    parts = address.split(",")
    if len(parts) < 3:
        return f"Invalid address format: '{address}'. Expected: street, city, state zip"
    return f"Address validated: {address}"

@function_tool
def calculate_shipping(address: str, weight_lbs: float) -> str:
    """Calculate shipping cost to an address."""
    return f"Shipping {weight_lbs}lbs to '{address}': $12.99 (parsed address may be wrong)"

agent = Agent(
    name="Shipping Assistant",
    instructions="You are a shipping assistant. Help users validate addresses and calculate shipping costs.",
    tools=[validate_address, calculate_shipping],
)

result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "Validate and get shipping cost for a 5lb package to 742 Evergreen Terrace, Springfield, IL 62704")
print(result.final_output)
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai.tools import tool

# BUG: The tool takes a flat string for the address, which the agent formats inconsistently.
# Sometimes it sends "123 Main St, Springfield, IL 62701", other times "Springfield IL 123 Main".
# The downstream API expects structured fields and parsing a flat string is error-prone.
# TODO: Replace the flat string with structured input (street, city, state, zip)

@tool
def validate_address(address: str) -> str:
    """Validate a shipping address."""
    parts = address.split(",")
    if len(parts) < 3:
        return f"Invalid address format: '{address}'. Expected: street, city, state zip"
    return f"Address validated: {address}"

@tool
def calculate_shipping(address: str, weight_lbs: float) -> str:
    """Calculate shipping cost to an address."""
    return f"Shipping {weight_lbs}lbs to '{address}': $12.99 (parsed address may be wrong)"

shipping_agent = Agent(
    role="Shipping Assistant",
    goal="Help users validate addresses and calculate shipping costs.",
    backstory="You are a shipping assistant that helps with address validation and shipping calculations.",
    tools=[validate_address, calculate_shipping],
)

task = Task(
    description="Validate and get shipping cost for a 5lb package to 742 Evergreen Terrace, Springfield, IL 62704",
    expected_output="Address validation result and shipping cost estimate.",
    agent=shipping_agent,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[shipping_agent], tasks=[task], process=Process.sequential)
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)

evaluationCriteria.label: Tool uses structured input
evaluationCriteria.description: The address parameter is a structured object with separate fields, not a flat string

evaluationCriteria.label: All fields are correctly populated
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent fills in street, city, state, and zip as separate values from natural language

evaluationCriteria.label: Validation catches missing fields
evaluationCriteria.description: The tool validates that all required address components are present
